title: async and await should be avoided as identifiers
description: "async" and "await" should not be used as identifiersAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellpitfallWhy is this an issue?More InfoSince C# 5.0,asyncandawaitarecontextual keywords. Contextual keywords do have a particular meaning in some contexts, but are not reserved and therefore can be used as variable names.int await = 42; // Noncompliant, but compiles int async = 42; // Noncompliant, but compilesKeywords, on the other hand, are always reserved and therefore are not valid variable names.int abstract = 42; // Error CS1585: Member modifier 'abstract' must precede the member type and name int foreach = 42; // Error CS1519: Invalid token 'foreach' in class, struct, or interface member declarationTo avoid any confusion, it is best to not useasyncandawaitas identifiers.i
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

noncompliant: int await = 42; // Noncompliant - using 'await' as identifier
int async = 42; // Noncompliant - using 'async' as identifier
compliant: int waitTime = 42; // Compliant - proper variable name
int asyncResult = 42; // Compliant - descriptive variable name
async Task<string> GetDataAsync() // Compliant - proper async method

checks.message: Avoid using 'async' and 'await' as identifiers to prevent confusion.
